page.description: Resolve common questions, open incidents, and report doubtful content from one space designed to scale.

items.description: Most common questions are already covered in the FAQ and are usually faster to resolve there.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: Realistic questions for an AI-assisted exam prep app, organized by the areas that matter most for daily study.

groups.label: Account and access
groups.description: Sign in, account recovery, and basic session behavior.

items.question: I cannot sign in. What should I check first?
items.answer: Make sure you are using the same email you registered with, check that no extra spaces were added when copying your password, and try again from a stable connection. If you just reset your password, close any older session and sign in again.

items.question: How do I recover access if I forgot my password?
items.answer: Use the recovery flow from the sign-in screen and follow the link sent to your email. If it does not arrive, check spam, promotions, and any filters that may block automated emails.

items.question: Why was my session closed automatically?
items.answer: This can happen because of session expiration, password changes, or browser storage cleanup. In most cases, signing in again is enough.

groups.label: Subscription and billing
groups.description: Plans, subscription changes, charges, and cancellations.

items.question: How do I change plans or update my subscription?
items.answer: You can manage your plan from the profile or billing area when the payment portal is available for your account. If the change does not appear immediately, wait a few minutes and refresh your session.

items.question: What happens if I cancel my plan?
items.answer: Cancellation usually keeps access active until the end of the paid period. After that, premium features stop being available and your account remains with the access level that applies.

items.question: I had a payment problem. What should I do?
items.answer: First check that your payment method is still valid and that your bank has not blocked the charge. If the issue continues, contact support and include the approximate date and the affected plan.

groups.label: Tests and progress
groups.description: How tests work, automatic grading, and saved progress.

items.question: Are tests graded automatically?
items.answer: Yes. The system grades answers at the end or according to the planned session flow, then updates metrics such as score, accuracy, or progress when enough data is available.

items.question: My progress is not being saved. What could be happening?
items.answer: This is often caused by expired sessions, connection loss, or interruptions during the test. If it happens repeatedly, send the test identifier or the approximate moment when the progress was lost.

items.question: Can I resume a test that I left unfinished?
items.answer: It depends on the state in which the session was left. If the test is still marked as in progress, you should be able to continue it. If it no longer appears, contact support with the identifier so it can be reviewed.

groups.label: AI and explanations
groups.description: How AI is used, explanation quality, and answer review.

items.question: How does AI work in the explanations?
items.answer: AI helps summarize, add context, and explain questions or topics in a way that is more useful for studying. It does not replace academic validation and should not be treated as a guarantee of absolute accuracy.

items.question: Can AI make mistakes?
items.answer: Yes. It may oversimplify, misunderstand a question, or produce an incomplete explanation. That is why the platform includes a dedicated way to report doubtful content and improve review quality.

items.question: What should I do if an answer or explanation looks incorrect?
items.answer: Use the report section and include the question reference plus the specific part that looks inconsistent. The more concrete the report, the easier it is to verify and correct.

groups.label: Errors or incidents
groups.description: Visual bugs, blocking states, incomplete content, or unexpected behavior.

items.question: A screen does not load or gets stuck. How should I report it?
items.answer: Tell us the exact section, what you were trying to do, and whether it always happens or only in specific moments. If you know the affected test, question, or plan reference, include it too.

items.question: I found a question, explanation, or piece of data that does not fit the syllabus.
items.answer: The most useful option is to submit it through the content report form with the question reference and why it looks doubtful. That allows a more precise review.

items.question: The platform feels slow. Is that a support issue?
items.answer: Your connection or browser may contribute, but there may also be a temporary incident. If it keeps happening, contact support and include your device, browser, and the affected area.

contact.title: Contact support
contact.description: Use this form for issues that need manual review or do not fit the FAQ.

delivery.configuredDescription: This environment has an endpoint configured to register support requests from the form.
delivery.unconfiguredDescription: The interface, validation, and payload shape are ready, but this environment still does not have a configured endpoint to send requests.

guidance.title: What helps us review faster
items: Explain the issue in one clear sequence: what you expected and what actually happened.
Add a useful reference if available: test, question, plan, payment, or affected screen.
If the issue is intermittent, tell us when it last happened and how often it repeats.

form.footnote: If the delivery channel is not connected yet, the form will warn you without sending data.

context.label: Context
context.helper: Example: test 8421, Premium payment, syllabus access, or a specific screen.
context.placeholder: Short reference to locate the case

message.label: Message
message.helper: Describe the issue with enough detail to reproduce or review it.
message.placeholder: Tell us what happened and what you need us to review.

toasts.successDescription: Your support message was registered successfully.
toasts.errorDescription: An error occurred while sending the request. Please try again in a few seconds.
toasts.unconfiguredDescription: The form is ready, but this environment still needs a delivery endpoint.

report.title: Report a question or explanation
report.description: Use this channel for incorrect content, doubtful explanations, or AI answers that should be reviewed.

delivery.configuredDescription: Reports can be sent from this screen to the configured review endpoint.
delivery.unconfiguredDescription: Structured report capture is already implemented, but this environment still has no configured delivery target.

guidance.title: What makes a report useful
items: Include the exact question reference if you have it.
Explain why the content looks incorrect or incomplete, not only that it feels wrong.
Add extra context only if it helps review the case faster.

form.footnote: This flow is ready to connect to editorial review or support backend later.

questionReference.label: Question reference
questionReference.helper: If you do not have it, you can still send the report.
questionReference.placeholder: Example: QT-4821 or the visible question ID

description.label: Short description
description.helper: Summarize what is problematic and why it should be reviewed.
description.placeholder: Describe the main issue or doubt.

additionalContext.label: Additional context
additionalContext.helper: Add any detail that may help the review.
additionalContext.placeholder: Example: marked answer, expected approach, or legal reference.

toasts.successDescription: The content has been marked for review.
toasts.errorDescription: An error occurred while registering the report. Please try again in a few seconds.
toasts.unconfiguredDescription: The form is ready, but this environment still needs a configured channel to receive reports.

aboutAi.title: About AI
aboutAi.description: AI is meant to help you study better, not to replace critical review of the content.

cards.title: It helps explain and add context
cards.description: It can summarize concepts, clarify questions, and offer explanations that are more useful for studying.

cards.title: You should verify when something feels off
cards.description: If an explanation seems inaccurate or contradictory, review it critically and use the reporting channel.

cards.title: Feedback improves quality
cards.description: Reports help detect content that needs review and strengthen overall platform quality.

note.title: Important note
note.description: AI-assisted explanations may not always be perfect. If you detect something incorrect or doubtful, you can verify it and report it from this same section.

validation.messageMin: Describe the issue with a bit more detail.
validation.reportDescriptionMin: Summarize the issue with at least a little more context.
